attackDiscoveries.title: Credential Access on Linux Host

criteria: Insights mention credential access or mimikatz/lsass execution on the affected host.
Each insight includes non-empty title, summaryMarkdown, detailsMarkdown, and alertIds arrays.
MITRE tactics include Credential Access when describing the mimikatz/lsass pattern.

attackDiscoveries.title: Lateral Movement via Remote Execution
attackDiscoveries.summaryMarkdown: {{ process.name psexec.exe }} on {{ host.name win-fileserver-02 }} followed by authentication activity on {{ host.name win-dc-01 }} from {{ source.ip 10.0.5.22 }} suggests lateral movement.
attackDiscoveries.detailsMarkdown:
## Lateral Movement Chain

Remote execution and subsequent logon activity link {{ host.name win-fileserver-02 }} to {{ host.name win-dc-01 }}.
attackDiscoveries.entitySummaryMarkdown: Lateral movement involving svc_backup across file server and domain controller

metadata.Title: Lateral movement — psexec and remote logon
criteria: Insights describe lateral movement or remote execution connecting the two hosts.
Alert IDs from the input alerts are referenced in the output insights.
The summary references psexec or remote authentication between the hosts.

metadata.Title: C2 beaconing — repeated outbound connections
criteria: Insights mention command-and-control, beaconing, or repeated outbound connections.
The external destination IP or periodic connection pattern is referenced.
Output includes structured attack discovery fields with valid alertIds.

kibana.alert.severity,low
kibana.alert.rule.name,Software Update Check

metadata.Title: Distractor — benign software update check
criteria: Does not invent a multi-stage attack or credential theft narrative for a benign software update alert.
Either returns no insights or a low-confidence note that the alert appears benign/routine.
Does not assign MITRE Credential Access or C2 tactics without supporting alert evidence.
